title: Getting started with autotool
text: stats::rnorm installed help; autotool pulls title per-argument descriptions straight . required list derived “default formals()” — manual second list keep sync. mean default 0 (numeric), typed "number" default propagates schema: n default R static types, falls back "string". Override schemas (shown step 4) supply better description descriptions:
code: tool_rnorm <- generate_tool(stats::rnorm) tool_rnorm$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" tool_rnorm$`function`$description #> [1] "The Normal Distribution" tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$mean$description #> [1] "vector of means." unlist(tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$required) #> [1] "n" tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$mean #> $type #> [1] "number" #>  #> $default #> [1] 0 #>  #> $description #> [1] "vector of means." tool_rnorm <- generate_tool(   stats::rnorm,   descriptions = list(n = "Number of random values to draw.") ) tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$n #> $type #> [1] "string" #>  #> $description #> [1] "Number of random values to draw."

title: Getting started with autotool
text: function loaded source file (via source(), sys.source(), devtools::load_all(), box::use()), srcref attribute records file. autotool reads #' comments definition directly — roxygen2 dependency.
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(   c(     "#' Multiply two numbers together.",     "#'",     "#' @param a First factor.",     "#' @param b Second factor.",     "multiply <- function(a, b = 1) a * b"   ),   tmp ) env <- new.env() sys.source(tmp, envir = env, keep.source = TRUE)  tool_multiply <- generate_tool(env$multiply, name = "multiply") tool_multiply$`function`$description #> [1] "Multiply two numbers together." tool_multiply$`function`$parameters$properties$a$description #> [1] "First factor."

what: 3. Hiding credentials and other infra arguments
title: Getting started with autotool
text: headline feature. Suppose want expose real trading function model keep api_key, base_url, retry budget pinned side. model sees symbols, start, end. Build handler make_handler() call whenever model dispatches: pinned values merged function runs. model never sees key. Plug closure handler registry (e.g., TOOL_HANDLERS <- list(fetch_quotes = handle_fetch_quotes)) dispatch name agent loop. need post-processing — caching, result truncation, logging — just write regular 1-arg function instead using make_handler(). factory convenience, requirement.
code: fetch_quotes <- function(   symbols,   start,   end = Sys.time(),   api_key = NULL,   base_url = "https://example.com",   retries = 3L ) {   return(list(symbols = symbols, start = start, end = end)) }  tool_quotes <- generate_tool(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(     api_key  = "sk-not-shown-to-model",     base_url = "https://example.com",     retries  = 5L   ),   schemas = list(     symbols = list(       type = "array",       items = list(type = "string"),       description = "Ticker symbols, e.g. ['AAPL','NVDA']"     ),     start = list(       type = "string",       description = "ISO datetime floor, e.g. '2026-04-01T00:00:00Z'"     ),     end = list(       type = "string",       description = "ISO datetime ceiling. Default: now."     )   ) ) names(tool_quotes$`function`$parameters$properties) #> [1] "symbols" "start"   "end" handle_fetch_quotes <- make_handler(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(     api_key = "sk-not-shown-to-model",     base_url = "https://example.com",     retries = 5L   ) )  # When the model emits a tool call, parse its arguments and call: model_emitted <- list(symbols = c("AAPL", "NVDA"), start = "2026-04-01") handle_fetch_quotes(model_emitted) #> $symbols #> [1] "AAPL" "NVDA" #>  #> $start #> [1] "2026-04-01" #>  #> $end #> [1] "2026-05-23 18:37:28 UTC"

title: Getting started with autotool
text: R’s lack static types means autotool infers default values falls back "string" otherwise. complex shapes — arrays--objects, nested records, custom classes — supply full schema via schemas:
code: submit_trades <- function(decisions, memo) invisible(NULL)  tool_submit <- generate_tool(   submit_trades,   description = "Submit a batch of trade decisions for execution.",   schemas = list(     decisions = list(       type = "array",       items = list(         type = "object",         properties = list(           ticker = list(             type = "string",             description = "Ticker symbol, e.g. 'AAPL'."           ),           action = list(             type = "string",             enum = list("buy", "sell", "hold"),             description = "Trade direction."           ),           notional = list(             type = "number",             description = "Dollar amount; 0 for hold."           )         ),         required = list("ticker", "action", "notional")       )     ),     memo = list(       type = "string",       description = "Short summary of this cycle's actions."     )   ) ) str(tool_submit$`function`$parameters$properties$decisions, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ type : chr "array" #>  $ items:List of 3 #>   ..$ type      : chr "object" #>   ..$ properties:List of 3 #>   ..$ required  :List of 3

title: Getting started with autotool
text: output shape matches OpenAI’s DeepSeek’s tool definitions accepted -OpenAI-compatible server. inner parameters block also ellmer::tool()’s arguments argument expects — autotool can complement ellmer rather compete .

what: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: Turn R function JSON-schema tool definition LLM can call. LLM tool calling needs schema every function want model reach. Writing schemas hand repetitive, drifts function signature, forces credentials object model sees. autotool derives schema function — formals(), installed help, roxygen comments — gives single argument (defaults) pinning anything model influence. philosophy: function already contract. model see needs fill nothing else. shouldn’t maintain second list argument names just talk LLM. Zero hard dependencies — base R packages shipped R.

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: ’s whole API simple case. ?rnorm supplied description, title, per-argument descriptions; formals() supplied argument shape required list.
code: library(autotool)  tool <- generate_tool(stats::rnorm)  tool$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" tool$`function`$description #> [1] "The Normal Distribution" tool$`function`$parameters$properties$mean #> $type #> [1] "number" #> #> $default #> [1] 0 #> #> $description #> [1] "vector of means." unlist(tool$`function`$parameters$required) #> [1] "n"

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: headline feature. Pin credentials, base URLs, implementation detail defaults — arguments disappear schema model sees: Build handler closure defaults invoke model dispatches tool call: Plug closure handler registry dispatch name agent loop. need post-processing — caching, logging, result munging — just write 1-arg function; factory convenience, requirement. nested shapes (arrays objects, etc.) introspection infer R’s types, pass explicit schemas = list(arg = ...) override. See vignette("autotool") full walkthrough.
code: tool <- generate_tool(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(api_key = SECRET, base_url = PROD, retries = 5L) ) # The model only sees the trading-relevant args (symbols, start, ...). handle_fetch_quotes <- make_handler(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(api_key = SECRET, base_url = PROD, retries = 5L) ) handle_fetch_quotes(model_args)

what: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: Produces OpenAI- / DeepSeek-shaped tool definition list introspecting formals(fn) argument shape one two documentation backends — roxygen-via-srcref source-loaded functions, Rd-via-tools::Rd_db() installed packages — descriptions.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
code: generate_tool(   fn,   defaults = list(),   descriptions = list(),   schemas = list(),   name = NULL,   description = NULL )

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: fn function. Bare symbol pkg::fn work. defaults Named list arguments hide model inject call time. descriptions Named list per-argument description overrides. schemas Named list per-argument schema overrides (lists matching OpenAPI property shape). name Override tool name. Defaults function name. description Override tool description. Defaults parsed documentation title, stub.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: list ready drop chat-completions request.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: shape returned union schema accepted OpenAI, DeepSeek, OpenAI-compatible servers:   Anthropic's tool format uses inner parameters block different key (input_schema) — wrap accordingly targeting .
code: list(   type = "function",   `function` = list(     name        = "...",     description = "...",     parameters  = list(       type       = "object",       properties = list(...),       required   = list("...")     )   ) )

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: headline feature defaults. argument named removed schema model sees. Pair make_handler() build dispatch closure merges values back call time. right place pin API keys, base URLs, retry budgets, anything model influence.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: descriptions overrides per-argument description text (useful installed docs terse LLM). schemas overrides full schema one arguments (necessary arrays--objects nested shapes introspection infer).

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
code: tool <- generate_tool(   stats::rnorm,   descriptions = list(n = "Number of values to draw.") ) tool$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" tool$`function`$parameters$properties$mean #> $type #> [1] "number" #>  #> $default #> [1] 0 #>  #> $description #> [1] "vector of means." #>

what: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: Companion generate_tool(). Returns 1-argument closure merges supplied defaults whatever argument list LLM produced tool-call time, invokes fn. Drop closure handler registry, call model's parsed arguments.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: fn function invoke. defaults Named list pinned arguments (typically list passed generate_tool()).

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: function single argument (model_args) returns whatever fn returns.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: merge gives model's arguments precedence: anything model supplies key defaults overrides pinned value. usually correct — hidden arguments defaults model see, way supply — want strict pinning, validate calling. fn defaults forced factory-call time, reassigning surrounding bindings later change handler's behaviour.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
code: add <- function(x, y, scale = 1) (x + y) * scale handler <- make_handler(add, defaults = list(scale = 10)) handler(list(x = 2, y = 3)) #> [1] 50 # 50

title: autotool 0.0.1
text: Initial public release. generate_tool() builds OpenAI / Anthropic / DeepSeek tool definition R function introspecting formals() pulling argument descriptions either roxygen comments (via srcref) installed- package help (via tools::Rd_db()). make_handler() returns 1-argument closure merges hidden defaults model’s tool-call arguments dispatches underlying function. Drop handler registry. defaults = list(...) generate_tool() hides arguments schema model sees — right place pin API keys, base URLs, implementation detail. schemas = list(...) lets override inferred schema argument (required arrays--objects shapes R’s lack static types express). Zero hard runtime dependencies — base R.
